political.s: No publicly available data on TaylorMade Golf Company's political donations or lobbying activities. As a private company owned by Centroid Investment Partners, political contribution disclosures are not required.

charity.s: TaylorMade supports community organizations through its Corporate Giving Program, focusing on health and vitality in communities served. The company supports organizations including The First Tee, Pro Kids Golf, and Boys and Girls Club, with employee volunteer programs and community engagement initiatives.
charity.focus: Youth golf development, community health, education

environment.s: TaylorMade has supplier responsibility standards requiring environmental sustainability and waste reduction. However, the company has not publicly disclosed carbon emissions data or formal climate reduction targets. Manufacturing includes recycled materials and energy efficiency practices, but lack of emissions reporting limits full assessment.

labor.s: Limited public data available. Glassdoor ratings show mixed employee satisfaction on compensation (3.3/5). The company appears to offer career development programs and was named one of America's Best Places to Work by Outside magazine in 2012. No significant OSHA violations or labor disputes reported in search results.

dei.s: TaylorMade Experience (event management division) states commitment to DEI practices. Main TaylorMade Golf Company has not disclosed comprehensive DEI initiatives, policy, or measurable diversity metrics publicly.

animals.s: Not applicable - golf equipment manufacturer does not conduct animal testing.

guns.s: Not applicable - manufactures golf equipment, not firearms.

privacy.s: TaylorMade has a privacy policy acknowledging data transmission security limitations. The company collects personal information for accounts and services, shares data with service providers, and notes data transfer to US servers. Privacy protections appear standard but limited transparency on data practices.

execPay.s: Limited data available. Comparably reports average TaylorMade Golf executive compensation of $226,795 annually, with executives ranging from $57,000 to $700,000. Average employee salary estimated at $80,083-$109,465. Executive-to-worker pay ratio not disclosed by company.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
